Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: NPA March Mania Plant Sale
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

March is a great time to buy plants from a select group of local small nurseries and specialty growers who sell an amazing collection of hard-to-find and old-time favorite perennials, shrubs, ferns, grasses and more. Your support helps these growers thrive and prosper, and in return they can grow even more special plants for our gardens! This is a ‘Farmer’s Market Style’ plant sale where you purchase plants directly from each seller – bring your own small wagon/cart to carry your purchases back to your car. The plant sale features plants from local growers. Visit the NPA Border after shopping! Email for more info.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Kameelah Janan Rasheed: we leak, we exceed
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

Kameelah Janan Rasheed \(b. 1985, East Palo Alto, CA; lives and works in Brooklyn, NY\) is a self-described “learner” who creates multi-media works that explore the poetics-pleasures-politics of Black knowledge production, information technologies, \[un\]learning, and belief formation. She often integrates found images and words alongside her own writing to create collaged wall installations and experimental video works. Across her work, Rasheed investigates the revision and life cycles of text, embracing expanded and embodied forms of writing and reading. we leak, we exceed will activate the unique volume and multiple vantage points of the Henry’s double-height gallery, drawing together threads from physics, Black critical thought, and information theory to create an immersive environment that interrogates the spatial and social implications of compression. A common process used in data storage, spatial organization, and information systems, compression abbreviates and collapses complex ideas into more simplified forms. Rasheed questions the way compression comes at the cost of nuance and creates unrecoverable losses. She draws parallels between the compression of information and the containment of people, both physically and through the structuring and defining of identities. Through a network of video, sound, and architectural mark-making, Rasheed proposes alternatively what she calls “an embrace of Black excess and expansion” as a liberatory practice.

item.title: Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

Event details

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

Event details

item.title: Kameelah Janan Rasheed: we leak, we exceed
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

Kameelah Janan Rasheed \(b. 1985, East Palo Alto, CA; lives and works in Brooklyn, NY\) is a self-described “learner” who creates multi-media works that explore the poetics-pleasures-politics of Black knowledge production, information technologies, \[un\]learning, and belief formation. She often integrates found images and words alongside her own writing to create collaged wall installations and experimental video works. Across her work, Rasheed investigates the revision and life cycles of text, embracing expanded and embodied forms of writing and reading. we leak, we exceed will activate the unique volume and multiple vantage points of the Henry’s double-height gallery, drawing together threads from physics, Black critical thought, and information theory to create an immersive environment that interrogates the spatial and social implications of compression. A common process used in data storage, spatial organization, and information systems, compression abbreviates and collapses complex ideas into more simplified forms. Rasheed questions the way compression comes at the cost of nuance and creates unrecoverable losses. She draws parallels between the compression of information and the containment of people, both physically and through the structuring and defining of identities. Through a network of video, sound, and architectural mark-making, Rasheed proposes alternatively what she calls “an embrace of Black excess and expansion” as a liberatory practice.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

item.title: Young Dragon: A Bruce Lee Story
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Seattle Children's Theatre, 201 Thomas Street, Seattle, WA

Before he was a legend, Bruce Lee was just a young man trying to figure it all out—right here in Seattle. This action-packed, soul-searching story follows the Young Dragon as he navigates a new country and finds his voice. Through movement, magic, and heart, we meet the human behind the icon—flawed, fierce, and full of possibility. Watch as Lee learns to clear his brain by mimicking the white noise of water and begins fusing martial arts with philosophy. From street fights in Hong Kong to teaching self-defense and chasing big dreams, Bruce’s journey reminds us that finding your way means first figuring out who you are. Come see how Seattle shaped him into a flexible, fluid, and flowing master—and how he reshaped the world.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Boren Banner Series: Camille Trautman
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Frye Art Museum, 704 Terry Ave, Seattle, WA

Every photograph is a reminder that the act of framing is never neutral. In Camille Trautman’s first solo museum exhibition in their hometown, the Seattle-born Duwamish artist uses photography and video to challenge colonial narratives and counter Indigenous erasure. The exhibition presents selections from their ongoing series The North American LCD—spectral self-portraits staged in varied natural landscapes. Rooted in Trautman’s process of coming out as transgender, the images show the artist’s body partially obscured by large LCD screens. The series examines how both landscape photography and digital media can shape or distort cultural identity, often by promoting or denying visibility.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Armory

Seattle Center Festál presents Seattle’s French Fest: A Celebration of French Speaking Cultures in partnership with France Education Northwest (FEN). The festival celebrates the diversity of traditions, ideas, dialects, and more that make up French-speaking cultures around the world. While France is often the most well-known French-speaking country for people in the U.S., there are many other countries and places all around the world where French is spoken.

item.title: Susan Meiselas: Crossings
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Photographic Center Northwest (PCNW), 900 12th Ave., Seattle, WA

First presented at the Art Institute of Chicago in 1990, this exhibition by Susan Meiselas intertwines documentary photographs of Central America during the 1970s and 1980s \(from Nicaragua and El Salvador\) along with panoramic images from the US/Mexico border taken in 1989. Additional photographs of the US/Mexico border taken in 2018 are also featured in a site-specific gallery installation. The exhibition invites viewers to see immigration in the context of economic and political crises in the region. Today, the work feels newly urgent. US immigration enforcement has expanded through militarization and constant surveillance, with leaders using fear-driven language to divide communities and legitimize brutal forms of control. These photographs ask us to look carefully, to recognize familiar patterns, and to hold on to our shared humanity across the globe. People move in search of safety, possibility, and a better life for their families, which is a universal hope that crosses borders, generations, and time.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: CROSSROADS BELLEVUE INVITES COMMUNITY TO ‘LIVE MUSIC AT THE MARKET STAGE
item.description:
Sunday, March 22, 2026 at 12:30 PM

Location: CROSSROADS BELLEVUE, 15600 NE 8th Street, Bellevue, WA

Crossroads Bellevue, a one-of-a-kind shopping, dining, and entertainment destination in the heart of Bellevue, Washington \(https://crossroadsbellevue.com\), invites the community to enjoy “Live Music at the Market Stage,” a series of free live musical performances held in the public market every Friday \(7 p.m. to 8:45 p.m.\), Saturday \(7 p.m. to 8:45 p.m.\), and Sunday \(12:30 p.m. to 2:30 p.m.\) now through the end of the year. Performances include an eclectic mix of acoustic guitar, Cuban music, jazz, local choirs and more. All performances are free of charge. To learn more about each upcoming performance, visit Market Stage | Crossroads Bellevue. While enjoying the music, guests are encouraged to dine at one of the center’s many restaurants. For more information about Crossroads Bellevue, visit https://crossroadsbellevue.com. WHEN:             Each Friday, Saturday and Sunday through the End of the Year -        Fridays: 7 p.m. - 8:45 p.m. -        Saturdays: 7 p.m. - 8:45 p.m. -        Sundays: 12:30 p.m. - 2:30 p.m.

Event details

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

item.title: Sturtevant's Ski-Ya-Later Sale: Up to 50% Off Ski and SnowboardGear
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Sturtevant's Ski Mart, 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA

PNW skiers and snowboarders—spring is the smartest time to upgrade! Sturtevant's \(part of Christy Sports\) is hosting the annual Ski-Ya-Later Sale from March 6-29, offering up to 50% off current-season skis, snowboards, boots, bindings, apparel, and more—perfect as spring storms bring late-season conditions and families plan ahead during spring break. Shop in-person at our Bellevue locations for expert tuning & repair, custom boot fitting, water sports rentals, and more: Sturtevant's – 13131 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.869.7669\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am- 6 pm Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.637.8958\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm Or head to Tacoma: Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 2220 So. 37th St., Tacoma, WA 98409 \(253.473.1134\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm. Great for families: Stop by for kids' fit checks, season rental info, and early purchases before fall pricing. All discounted items are final sale; selection varies by store. Also shop online at christysports.com or sturtevants.com. Upgrade your gear locally this spring—visit Sturtevant's Bellevue or Tacoma today. Dates: March 6 – March 29, 2026 \(Progressive discounts: Up to 30% off March 6-12 with the best selection; up to 40% off March 13-19; up to 50% off March 20-29 for the deepest savings\)

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: Endless Opportunities: Volunteer Birthday Dreams
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Stroum Jewish Community Center, 3801 East Mercer Way, Mercer Island, WA

Join us in bringing joy, dignity, and celebration to children experiencing homelessness through this Birthday Dreams service project. Birthday Dreams is a nonprofit organization dedicated to bringing hope, joy, and a sense of self-worth to homeless children with the gift of a birthday party, partnering with more than 80 shelters across the Puget Sound region to ensure every child feels loved and remembered on their special day. We’ll come together to assemble goody bags and curate other birthday items that are delivered to local shelters and families. These items help create a complete birthday experience—from party favors and treats to thoughtful supplies that make each child’s celebration feel magical and meaningful.

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

item.title: Volpone
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: ACT Theatre, 700 Union Street, Seattle, WA

Volpone \(The Fox\), a wealthy Venetian, lusts for yet more gold, and with the help of his devious brilliant servant Mosca \(The Fly\), delights in finding ways to steal it from his wealthy, corrupt Venetian neighbors. Ben Jonson’s renowned 1606 comedy takes us on a wild funny ride with one of the greatest plots in all theatre through a greedy mad society where self-interest is the only real value, and deception the only truth. And where the greatest pleasure of all is…THE GRIFT.

item.title: Geeks Who Drink Trivia at Stone's Throw Coffee & Market
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Stone's Throw Coffee & Market, 1218 N 45th St, Seattle, WA

Geeks Who Drink Trivia is an authentic, homegrown pub quiz, where useless knowledge means everything. Our general knowledge quizzes cover everything from literature to lit memes, from pop culture to the history of PopTarts—crafted in creative, multimedia rounds that’ll keep you thinking and laughing all night. Bring your cleverest friends \(or your dumbest, if you want to look extra smart\), or play solo and meet some fresh faces. Free & fun to play, all you need is your phone! Join us at Stone's Throw Coffee & Market every Monday, where the fun starts at 7pm. To learn more, visit geekswhodrink.com.

Event details

item.title: Pearly Drops
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Neumos, 925 E Pike St, Seattle, WA

Barboza Presents The Voices Are Coming Back Tour PEARLY DROPS with Special Guests MON, 23 MAR 2026 at 07:00PM PDT Ages: 21 & Over Doors Open: 07:00PM OnSale: Fri, 29 Aug 2025 at 10:00AM PDT Announcement: Tue, 26 Aug 2025 at 10:00AM PDT Painting with a bold, slightly macabre palette, Pearly Drops’ Sandra Tervonen and Juuso Malin create a realm of strange, softly smudged pop. On the Helsinki act’s third full-length, The Voices Are Coming Back, fantasy and the mundane tangle into an alluring web of mystery. The record — issued by forward-thinking New York City label Music Website — centers on an age-old tale: the saga of Hollywood hopefuls. Branching beyond their home of Finland, Pearly Drops are an emerging fixture of the global indie-electronic scene. The act wraps sweet, dreamy melodies in futuristic hues. It’s fitting that Tervonen and Malin share backgrounds in music production and collaborated on sound design efforts before morphing into an artist project. Pearly Drops’ 2020 debut, Call For Help, and its 2023 follow up, A Little Disaster, introduced a mesmerizing formula — blending misty rock and electronic elements. The pair’s often playful, grungy fashion sense enhances these chic sonics, with all photos and styling handled in house. Pearly Drops has received a Teosto Prize — one of the Nordic countries’ most prestigious music awards. The duo has collaborated with artists such as Vitesse X and KROY, and their own songs have been covered and remixed by acts including XL Recordings' Nourished by Time and American dream-pop favorites Dream, Ivory. On The Voices Are Coming Back, Tervonen and Malin swapped the familiarity of Finland for the peculiar glamour of Los Angeles. “We'd always imagined LA through the lens of David Lynch: saturated in surrealism, a little menacing, a little magical — and that’s exactly how it felt. A puzzling, but beautiful cultural shock,” Tervonen and Malin reflect on the city. The bizarre energy of Southern California beams bright on album opener “Delusional On Sunset Boulevard.” “I think I just got older / Growing cold and dumber too / No pat on my shoulder / I ride my horse through Hollywood,” Tervonen’s elven vocals skate around vast chords and the strobe of a synth lead. Amid the sprawl, Pearly Drops encountered kindred spirits including Australian group Cub Sport and photographer Tatiana Bruening \(aka illumitati\), who were invited to contribute to The Voices Are Coming Back. True to their highly attentive ethos, Pearly Drops and these creative partners crafted the entire record themselves. "Sonically, we wanted the album to sound like a fictional reality, with vocals coming from, for example, a sewer and overheated analog modular synthesizers seamlessly blending with guitar-driven pop music,” Tervonen and Malin share. “We don’t identify as an indie rock band, but we don’t feel like an electronic act either. We composed, wrote, produced and mixed the entire album just the two of us with our own hands. Sandra has also painted all the cover arts by hand. For our artistic expression, it’s important to see through the whole process ourselves." Call For Help pondered loneliness and A Little Disaster grappled with death. The Voices Are Coming Back retains Pearly Drops’ signature introspection, though it stands as the duo’s most narrative full-length to date. “At its heart, this album is about the emotional unraveling that can happen when you chase a dream too hard—when fantasy and ambition start to bleed into your sense of identity. We used the idea of the “Hollywood dream” as a narrative frame, but really, it’s a metaphor for the deeper psychological shifts that come with longing and disillusionment. It’s about getting lost—not just in a place, but in yourself. Coming from Helsinki, there's always been a sense of distance or introspection in our music. This time, we wanted to embrace that and stretch it into something even more surreal and emotionally complex. The album isn’t just storytelling—it’s autofiction. The characters, the landscapes, the dream logic—they’re all extensions of our personal experiences as artists navigating uncertainty, burnout, and ambition in a world that’s constantly shifting.” The album draws a relatable, yet otherworldly picture adrift in uncertainty, drawn by the age-old allure of reinvention. Instead of glamor, they find themselves lost in the woods of Franklin Hills. Hiding behind a tree from mystical voices one moment, the next they’ve transformed into mermaids in the sewers under Sunset Boulevard. Time melts away on drives around the eastern Santa Monica Mountains. In the end, the only way back is through a forest in Silver Lake. Despite the storybook universe it conjures, Pearly Drops cite The Voices Are Coming Back as a work of autofiction. Still, grounded influences are present on the record, themes of anguish lingering beneath the surface. This pain is conveyed through voice memos from Bruening on the songs “Shallow,” “Demonlover,” and “Cocoon & Tatiana’s Lament.” Meanwhile, Cub Sport’s ethereal opera defines "Mermaid." “Laying in my mess / Mermaid in her manhole / It’s gone bad / I’m drinking the poison / I am seeing visions while awake,” Tervonen breathily sings in the first verse of the song over a thicket of chorus-heavy fretwork. “Ratgirl” continues “Mermaid”’s ride down Talmadge Street, inspired by fantasy, haunted by the echo of a promise that never arrived and made gripping by a dancey, electroclash pulse. “End Credits” serves as a spiritual intermission, Tervonen singing about a screen fading to black over a new wave instrumental. Late album highlight, “Pillow Face,” is a muffled scream about the dread of beauty. From start to finish, The Voices Are Coming Back is united by upbeat rhythms and gossamer textures. As The Voices Are Coming Back’s journey nears a close, Pearly Drops are emotionally unraveled. “Only believe in / My digital feelings / Still reaching for the / Undo in the void,” Tervonen sings on the closing track, “Silver Lake Mystery Forest.” “It feels like with this third album, we bottled up that feeling of losing your mind and your identity when trying to chase your dreams — whatever they happen to be,” Tervonen and Malin muse. Across this feverish vision of a record, the uncertainty and burnout of everyday life manifest in uncanny, albeit deeply human ways. Accessibility Disclaimer: Barboza is not wheelchair accessible. Important Information: \* This event is 21 & over. Valid ID is required. Minors will not be permitted entry. \* Doors open at the listed event time. \* Event start time is generally one hour after doors open but is subject to change without notice. \* All tickets are nonrefundable with the exception of event cancellation. \* Support acts are subject to change. \* Orders in violation of the published ticket limit are subject to cancellation without notice.

Event details

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

item.title: Sturtevant's Ski-Ya-Later Sale: Up to 50% Off Ski and SnowboardGear
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Sturtevant's Ski Mart, 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA

PNW skiers and snowboarders—spring is the smartest time to upgrade! Sturtevant's \(part of Christy Sports\) is hosting the annual Ski-Ya-Later Sale from March 6-29, offering up to 50% off current-season skis, snowboards, boots, bindings, apparel, and more—perfect as spring storms bring late-season conditions and families plan ahead during spring break. Shop in-person at our Bellevue locations for expert tuning & repair, custom boot fitting, water sports rentals, and more: Sturtevant's – 13131 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.869.7669\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am- 6 pm Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.637.8958\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm Or head to Tacoma: Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 2220 So. 37th St., Tacoma, WA 98409 \(253.473.1134\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm. Great for families: Stop by for kids' fit checks, season rental info, and early purchases before fall pricing. All discounted items are final sale; selection varies by store. Also shop online at christysports.com or sturtevants.com. Upgrade your gear locally this spring—visit Sturtevant's Bellevue or Tacoma today. Dates: March 6 – March 29, 2026 \(Progressive discounts: Up to 30% off March 6-12 with the best selection; up to 40% off March 13-19; up to 50% off March 20-29 for the deepest savings\)

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Sturtevant's Ski Mart, 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA

PNW skiers and snowboarders—spring is the smartest time to upgrade! Sturtevant's \(part of Christy Sports\) is hosting the annual Ski-Ya-Later Sale from March 6-29, offering up to 50% off current-season skis, snowboards, boots, bindings, apparel, and more—perfect as spring storms bring late-season conditions and families plan ahead during spring break. Shop in-person at our Bellevue locations for expert tuning & repair, custom boot fitting, water sports rentals, and more: Sturtevant's – 13131 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.869.7669\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am- 6 pm Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.637.8958\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm Or head to Tacoma: Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 2220 So. 37th St., Tacoma, WA 98409 \(253.473.1134\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm. Great for families: Stop by for kids' fit checks, season rental info, and early purchases before fall pricing. All discounted items are final sale; selection varies by store. Also shop online at christysports.com or sturtevants.com. Upgrade your gear locally this spring—visit Sturtevant's Bellevue or Tacoma today. item.title: Mindfulness Walk | Pioneer Park
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 11 AM

Mindfulness Walk Taking inspiration from the Japanese idea of shinrin-yoku \(forest bathing\), we will be hosting a series of mindfulness walks in Pioneer Park. Mindfulness walking in the forest has shown to have a myriad of benefits from improving heart health to reducing stress \(https://www.npr.org/2023/08/22/1195337204/a-guide-to-forest-bathing\). Forest Steward Marilyn Cornwell \(https://www.soulscapes.us/\) will be lea ding an hour-long guided walk through the park. Please join us if your cur ious about mindfulness, want to learn more about and experience healing powers of being in nature, and/or if you want to deepen your connection with Pioneer Park and others. When: Tuesday, March 24th, 11am-12pm Where: Pioneer Park SE, Meet at the corner of Island Crest Way and SE 68th St. Follow VOLUNTEER EVENT Signs What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes and shoes you will comfortable walking and standing in, as well as water. Registration: https://volunteersignup.org/9XTJA Opens in new window PDF Download Word Download Excel Download PowerPoint Download Document Download

item.title: Hyper-Local Happy Hour (Non-alcoholic)
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Calvary: The Hill, 1107 E Howell St, Seattle, WA

Get to know your hyper-local Capitol Hill neighbors around Cal Anderson Park in an open house style, non-alcoholic happy hour!  Enjoy non-alcoholic beers and yummy mocktails, along with a charcuterie spread, all inside a 120-year-old historic Capitol Hill church building. These happy hours will be from 4-5:30pm every 4th Tuesday through June  January 27th February 24th March 24th April 28th May 26th June 23rd \*Please note that ADA access is available into the happy hour area, but not to the restrooms unfortunately \(very old building!\)

item.title: Discount Movie Night Project Hail Mary
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Cinemark Totem Lake and XD, Wells Fargo, 12516 Totem Lake Blvd NE, Kirkland, WA

This looks like a fun and inspirational sci-fi move \(along the lines of "The Martian" I'm thinking\). I've purchased a bunch of tickets in the middle of the theater so we can sit together in the best D-BOX seats for us again to really get into the movie! We'll also have regular seats for the 6:25pm showing. ~Claudette ️ TRAILER: https://youtu.be/P0XN3-n-2Lo?si=dUcm4R5RjCPlZ3tM ️ TICKETS: D-BOX - $17.00 Regular - $11.50 Reimburse me for a ticket via: Eventbrite \(fees apply\): https://www.eventbrite.com/e/movie-night-project-hail-mary-tickets-1983694348195 Or get your own - movie buffs, sign up for membership for perks at https://refer.cinemark.com/Claudette37. Don't go by the RSVPs to see if there are still seats with the group. Check comments for updates - I'll let you know when I don't have any more left. No need to ask if I still have tickets. Please arrive no later than 10 mins before the showing time or contact me to let me know your ETA, otherwise we'll enter without you and you'll lose your ticket. Refunds will ONLY be issued if this event is rescheduled, cancelled or if you send in a reimbursement and I no longer have tickets. WE WILL NOT BE OFFERING REFUNDS ON THIS EVENT. IF YOU CAN NO LONGER ATTEND AND CANCEL ANY TIME AFTER YOU RSVP, YOU WILL NEED TO SELL YOUR SPOT TO SOMEONE ELSE. Let's meet in the sofa lounge behind the elevator. If people get there before me, ask others if they are with meetup. This is a joint venture with Eastside Social Club on meetup. More people may be attending than what you see RSVP'd here. https://www.meetup.com/eastsidesocialclub/events/312570417

item.title: MOHAI Mercantile Literary Series - Seattle's Locks and Ship Canal
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

Register ➞ https://bit.ly/3NbLLJX MOHAI Mercantile is proud to host the official book launch event for Seattle’s Locks and Ship Canal with local historians David B. Williams and Jennifer Ott! This portable guide from UW Press and HistoryLink is an introduction to the engineering marvel of the Ballard locks, and a primer on the history of the ship canal. Hear from the authors about the tumultuous history of one of Seattle’s defining projects, and the dramatic changes it brought to the city’s economy, neighborhoods, and natural environment. Doors open at 6 pm for music and mingling. Food and drink available for purchase. Conversation and Q&A begin at 7 pm, followed by a book signing with the authors.

item.title: Destination Roadhouse -- Jazz Jam Night
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Roadhouse, 19955 28th Ave S, SeaTac, WA

Join us every Tuesday night for **The Roadhouse Jazz Jam**, where the city’s best musicians — and brave newcomers — come together to celebrate the spirit of live jazz. Bring your instrument, your voice, or just your love for great music. The night starts with a house band set, then the stage opens up for an unforgettable jam session filled with swing, soul, and spontaneity. 🎵 Smooth vibes • 🎺 Live improvisation Come play, listen, and feel the groove — the Tuesday Jazz Jam is where the magic happens.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: IMPROV 100 TUESDAY - Intro to Improv: Say Yes!, SPRING'26
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley, Seattle, WA

Say yes! Learn to listen, connect, play and use your own life to create stories with others! An 8 week intro to improvised theatre! Discover Unexpected Productions' philosophy of improvisation, developed over our 43 years of improvising in Seattle! You'll learn how to actively listen, to use your own life to create collaborative stories, and you'll work on the fundamental principles of improvisation: what it means to say, "Yes, And", how to tell stories that include everyone in the room, how to stop worrying about failing, and the games and concepts behind what we do on stages around the world! Under the guidance of one of our best Seattle improv teachers, you will learn to:· -Be a Better Listener -Conquer Fears -Spark Creativity -Meet New People & Make New Friends -Become a Better Problem Solver -Gain Confidence· -Fail With Grace \(and Maybe Even Enjoy It\) -Say Yes More in Your Life -Get Out of Your Comfort Zone -Become a Better Public Speaker -Discover More About Yourself -...and have a blast doing it! Time and Dates : Tuesdays for eight \(8\) weeks, from March 24 to May 22, 7pm to 9:30pm NOTE: Attendance at the first session of your course is mandatory as it sets the agenda for the entire quarter. If you think you will have to miss the first class, please consider registering for the next quarter. No Prerequisites. All students begin at this level, even those with existing improv experience. It’s a prerequisite to the other levels. Location: 5700 6th Ave St, Suite 109, Umbrella Room, Seattle, WA 98108 \(In Georgetown, south of downtown, with plenty of parking.\) Maximum Students: 16 Cost: Ticket price $295.00 Age: 18 and above \*\*Cancellation policy: If you must cancel before the first class, we can only offer credit towards the same level class next quarter. We are unable to offer refunds. If you cancel after the first class we cannot offer credit towards a future class. Again, we are unable to offer refunds.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Chuckle Bucket Tuesday @ Club Comedy Seattle (Open Mic)
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Club Comedy Seattle, 328 15th Ave E, Seattle, WA

Club Comedy Seattle hosts Chuckle Bucket Tuesday each week. The luck of the bucket rules the night. Comics drop their name in and hope to be drawn for one of the spots on the list to tell jokes. It's fun, fast-paced and will keep you laughing hard on your Tuesday night. Door 7:00PM/Show 7:30PM Admission: $7 \+ $0.35 City Of Seattle Admission Tax 21\+ With Valid ID REQUIRED for entry. NO REFUNDS. FYI: If you have a large party it's easier for us to seat you all together if you purchase all the tickets under one name. . Club Comedy Seattle FAQ Is there an age/ID requirement? Yes. We are strictly 21\+ and valid physical ID is REQUIRED For Entry Is Seating First Come/First Served? Yes. The room is all two top tables and we seat guests in the order in which they arrive. Can I reserve a table for a group? We do not take reservations for tables, but groups can arrive early to secure seating together. Are tickets available at the door if an event is sold out online? If an event is sold out online there are no tickets available at the door. What are the club’s hours of operation? The club is open Tuesday to Sunday. Doors open a half hour before each show. What is the Parking like? Generally there is street parking available in the blocks surrounding the club. There are also affordable parking lots on 16th Ave E right around the block from the club along with other easy options in the neighborhood Is there a dress code? There is no formal dress code, but we recommend smart casual attire. Can I bring food or drinks? Outside food and drinks are not allowed. We have a full bar and a menu available for your enjoyment. What if I can't make it and need to cancel my ticket? All ticket sales are final. However, if you can’t make it, please contact us through Eventbrite in advance, and we’ll do our best to accommodate you. Will there be an opener for the headliner? Yes, headliner shows feature a lineup of comedians, including a host, an opener, and the headliner. Will the comics talk to me like I see in online videos all the time? Most likely they won't. Crowd work videos are popular for posting online, but crowd work doesn't generally happen at the online frequency in live comedy show situations. Some comics do engage the crowd as part of their regular set and it's generally always fun and more about making fun of themselves than making fun of the crowd. How can I get on the Cheap Laughs Open Mic On Wednesdays? Submissions for spots on the Wednesday Cheap Laughs Open Mic are accepted only on the Tuesday before between 12:00AM and 11:59PM via email at openmic@clubcomedyseattle.com Do you offer group discounts? Yes, we offer discounts for groups of ten or more. Please contact us for more details. Can I meet the comedians after the show? Meet-and-greet opportunities vary by event. Please ask staff for details after the show.

item.title: IMPROV 100 TUESDAY - Intro to Improv: Say Yes!, SPRING'26
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley, Seattle, WA

Say yes! Learn to listen, connect, play and use your own life to create stories with others! An 8 week intro to improvised theatre! Discover Unexpected Productions' philosophy of improvisation, developed over our 43 years of improvising in Seattle! You'll learn how to actively listen, to use your own life to create collaborative stories, and you'll work on the fundamental principles of improvisation: what it means to say, "Yes, And", how to tell stories that include everyone in the room, how to stop worrying about failing, and the games and concepts behind what we do on stages around the world! Under the guidance of one of our best Seattle improv teachers, you will learn to:· -Be a Better Listener -Conquer Fears -Spark Creativity -Meet New People & Make New Friends -Become a Better Problem Solver -Gain Confidence· -Fail With Grace \(and Maybe Even Enjoy It\) -Say Yes More in Your Life -Get Out of Your Comfort Zone -Become a Better Public Speaker -Discover More About Yourself -...and have a blast doing it! Time and Dates : Tuesdays for eight \(8\) weeks, from March 24 to May 22, 7pm to 9:30pm NOTE: Attendance at the first session of your course is mandatory as it sets the agenda for the entire quarter. If you think you will have to miss the first class, please consider registering for the next quarter. No Prerequisites. All students begin at this level, even those with existing improv experience. It’s a prerequisite to the other levels. Location: 5700 6th Ave St, Suite 109, Umbrella Room, Seattle, WA 98108 \(In Georgetown, south of downtown, with plenty of parking.\) Maximum Students: 16 Cost: Ticket price $295.00 Age: 18 and above \*\*Cancellation policy: If you must cancel before the first class, we can only offer credit towards the same level class next quarter. We are unable to offer refunds. If you cancel after the first class we cannot offer credit towards a future class. Again, we are unable to offer refunds.

item.title: Chuckle Bucket Tuesday @ Club Comedy Seattle (Open Mic)
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Club Comedy Seattle, 328 15th Ave E, Seattle, WA

Club Comedy Seattle hosts Chuckle Bucket Tuesday each week. The luck of the bucket rules the night. Comics drop their name in and hope to be drawn for one of the spots on the list to tell jokes. It's fun, fast-paced and will keep you laughing hard on your Tuesday night. Door 7:00PM/Show 7:30PM Admission: $7 \+ $0.35 City Of Seattle Admission Tax 21\+ With Valid ID REQUIRED for entry. NO REFUNDS. FYI: If you have a large party it's easier for us to seat you all together if you purchase all the tickets under one name. . Club Comedy Seattle FAQ Is there an age/ID requirement? Yes. We are strictly 21\+ and valid physical ID is REQUIRED For Entry Is Seating First Come/First Served? Yes. The room is all two top tables and we seat guests in the order in which they arrive. Can I reserve a table for a group? We do not take reservations for tables, but groups can arrive early to secure seating together. Are tickets available at the door if an event is sold out online? If an event is sold out online there are no tickets available at the door. What are the club’s hours of operation? The club is open Tuesday to Sunday. Doors open a half hour before each show. What is the Parking like? Generally there is street parking available in the blocks surrounding the club. There are also affordable parking lots on 16th Ave E right around the block from the club along with other easy options in the neighborhood Is there a dress code? There is no formal dress code, but we recommend smart casual attire. Can I bring food or drinks? Outside food and drinks are not allowed. We have a full bar and a menu available for your enjoyment. What if I can't make it and need to cancel my ticket? All ticket sales are final. However, if you can’t make it, please contact us through Eventbrite in advance, and we’ll do our best to accommodate you. Will there be an opener for the headliner? Yes, headliner shows feature a lineup of comedians, including a host, an opener, and the headliner. Will the comics talk to me like I see in online videos all the time? Most likely they won't. Crowd work videos are popular for posting online, but crowd work doesn't generally happen at the online frequency in live comedy show situations. Some comics do engage the crowd as part of their regular set and it's generally always fun and more about making fun of themselves than making fun of the crowd. How can I get on the Cheap Laughs Open Mic On Wednesdays? item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

item.title: Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

Event details

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Sturtevant's Ski Mart, 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA

PNW skiers and snowboarders—spring is the smartest time to upgrade! Sturtevant's \(part of Christy Sports\) is hosting the annual Ski-Ya-Later Sale from March 6-29, offering up to 50% off current-season skis, snowboards, boots, bindings, apparel, and more—perfect as spring storms bring late-season conditions and families plan ahead during spring break. Shop in-person at our Bellevue locations for expert tuning & repair, custom boot fitting, water sports rentals, and more: Sturtevant's – 13131 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.869.7669\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am- 6 pm Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 13219 NE 20th St., Bellevue, WA 98005 \(425.637.8958\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm Or head to Tacoma: Sturtevant's Ski Mart – 2220 So. 37th St., Tacoma, WA 98409 \(253.473.1134\) Hours: Mon-Fri 10 am-7 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm. Great for families: Stop by for kids' fit checks, season rental info, and early purchases before fall pricing. All discounted items are final sale; selection varies by store. Also shop online at christysports.com or sturtevants.com. Upgrade your gear locally this spring—visit Sturtevant's Bellevue or Tacoma today. Dates: March 6 – March 29, 2026 \(Progressive discounts: Up to 30% off March 6-12 with the best selection; up to 40% off March 13-19; up to 50% off March 20-29 for the deepest savings\)

item.title: Guest Stylist Pop-up Event
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 11 AM

Description Nic of Ends of the Earth, Micki Yeates of Hatch Hair and Company, and Kitt Spata are teaming up for a queer haircut day Wednesday, March 25th 11am - 5pm. From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Boren Banner Series: Camille Trautman
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Frye Art Museum, 704 Terry Ave, Seattle, WA

Every photograph is a reminder that the act of framing is never neutral. In Camille Trautman’s first solo museum exhibition in their hometown, the Seattle-born Duwamish artist uses photography and video to challenge colonial narratives and counter Indigenous erasure. The exhibition presents selections from their ongoing series The North American LCD—spectral self-portraits staged in varied natural landscapes. Rooted in Trautman’s process of coming out as transgender, the images show the artist’s body partially obscured by large LCD screens. The series examines how both landscape photography and digital media can shape or distort cultural identity, often by promoting or denying visibility.

From SEAtoday - All Events
